phase_2.correlations: Strong negative correlation across available modalities: noisy environment (79.39 dBA) combined with continuous non-stationary motion creates a clear pattern of distraction. The brief 0.05-minute (3-second) session duration itself suggests this was not a genuine study attempt but rather incidental recording during transit or movement through a noisy area.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: This session shows no evidence of concentration. The combination of high ambient noise, active movement, and extremely short duration (3 seconds) indicates the user was in transit or briefly passing through an environment, not engaged in focused study activity. All available signals point to complete absence of study concentration.

phase_3.recommendations: 1) Seek a quiet, stationary environment with ambient noise below 45 dBA before starting study sessions. 2) Ensure you are seated and settled before beginning focused work - avoid studying while in motion or during transitions. 3) Aim for minimum session durations of 15-25 minutes to establish genuine concentration periods rather than brief recordings.
